entities.name: PEARL MILLET
entities.description: Pennisetum glaucum cv. ICTP 8203, the crop studied for water-use efficiency

entities.name: TRANSPIRATIONAL WATER-USE EFFICIENCY
entities.description: The efficiency of water use based on transpiration, denoted as WUET

entities.name: PHOSPHORUS AVAILABILITY
entities.description: The level of phosphorus (P) provided to the plants in the soil

entities.name: WATER STRESS
entities.description: The condition of limited water availability imposed on the plants

entities.name: OUTDOOR POT EXPERIMENT
entities.description: Experiments conducted in covered pots under semiarid conditions

entities.name: GROWTH CHAMBER EXPERIMENT
entities.description: Controlled environment experiments to study WUET and P levels

entities.name: SEMIARID CONDITIONS
entities.description: The environmental conditions under which outdoor pots were grown

entities.name: BETIS SAND
entities.description: A sandy, siliceous, thermic Psammentic Paleustalf soil used in the study

entities.name: DRY MATTER
entities.description: The mass of the plant excluding water, denoted as DM

entities.name: CUMULATIVE TRANSPIRATION
entities.description: The total amount of water transpired by the plant over time

entities.name: GAS EXCHANGE WATER-USE EFFICIENCY
entities.description: The ratio of leaf net photosynthetic rate to transpiration, denoted as WUEgas

entities.name: PHOTOSYNTHETIC PHOTON FLUX DENSITY
entities.description: The intensity of light available for photosynthesis, denoted as PPFD

entities.name: SOIL FERTILITY CONSTRAINTS
entities.description: Limitations in soil nutrients that affect crop production

relationships.description: Significant main and interactive effects on WUET

P level increased the slope of the curve relating DM to cumulative transpiration

PPFD levels influenced the increase of WUEgas depending on P levels
